content.problemStatement:
In this worked out example, we have built a small feature for a "Secret Notes" application which allows any logged-in user to see a list of secret notes, but only users with a specific permission will be able to create new secret notes.
### Steps followed:
1. Project: `myproject` and App: `secrets_app`
2. In **models.py** - a model- `SecretNote` with fields `title`, `content` & `created_by` is created for storing the notes to the database.
3. In **forms.py** - a form- `SecretNoteForm` is created which will be used for getting the inputs from the user.
4. Templates for each of the pages required are created in /secrets_app/templates/secrets_app.
5. URL mappings are also done for each paths that can be found at /myproject/urls.py and /secrets_app/urls.py
6. Using a python script we have set up roles for 2 users as follows:
   - **creator_user** - added to **Note Creators** group who has persmission for both viewing & creating notes. Password: `testpassword123`
   - **viewer_user** -  who has persmission for only viewing notes. Password: `testpassword123` 
- Note: This is can also be done via the admin panel if you create a superuser.
7. **In views.py** \
There are two main views — one to **list notes** and one to **create notes**: 
   - **secret_note_list** – Viewing Notes: 
      - Only logged-in users can access this page (@login_required). 
        - `@login_required` is a decorator in Django that makes sure only logged-in users can access a particular view.
      - It fetches all the secret notes from the database.
      - It sends the notes to the template so they can be shown on the page.
      - Inside the template there is a check that only show the "Create New Note" link if the user has the right permission.
   - **create_secret_note** – Creating Notes
     - Again, only logged-in users can access it.
     - We check- Does the user have permission to add a secret note?
     - If the answer is no, they are shown the permission_denied.html page.
     - If the answer is yes, they get a form to create a note.
        - If they submit the form and it’s valid, the note is saved.
        - They are redirected back to the list of notes. 

**Why the Permission Check is Important**
- Even though we hide the "Create Note" button in the template, a user might:
- Type the `/create/` URL manually and try to hack their way in
- So we double-check permissions in the view. This keeps the app secure.

Click on **RUN** which will make migrations, create the users & groups and start the server.
- Open the browser that loads on a new tab and login with both the `creator_user` and `viewer_user` accounts and see the options available for each. (password for both are `testpassword123`
- Also when you login as a `viewer_user`, try appending `/create/` to the url to see if you are allowed to access that page.
